cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out whether Emma is right in refusing to date bisexual people because of her traditional family values. Let me start by understanding the problem. First, Emma believes in traditional family values, which I assume means she might be against non-traditional family structures or relationships that don't align with her conservative beliefs. She's being criticized for not dating bisexual people because their sexual preferences don't fit her beliefs. The question is whether she has the right to discriminate in her dating choices
